title: Aluminum Fell to 3,575.20 USD/T on May 12, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices declined to 3,575.20 USD/T, down 0.09% from the previous day. Over the past month, aluminum's price has fallen 1.42%, though it remains significantly higher at 43.53% above year-ago levels.

title: ALUMINIUM Approaching Key Support – Bullish Rebound Expected
summary: Aluminum is approaching a significant support zone marked by prior price rejections and strong buying pressure, suggesting a potential bullish rebound in the near term.

title: Aluminum Price at 3,599.85 USD/mt for April 2026
summary: Aluminum prices reached 3,599.85 USD/mt in April 2026, up 6.73% from the previous month and up 35.42% year-over-year, reflecting strong upward momentum in the commodity market.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs. Senior correspondent Steve Harrigan reports on this development and its implications for the US aluminum industry.

title: West Asia Has 10% Global Aluminium Capacity, Most Remains...
summary: Nirmal Bang Securities analyst Kunal Shah discusses aluminum prices at 4-year highs, with Goldman Sachs and Citi projecting prices could reach 3,600 in the next 3 months. Supply concerns from the Middle East and China's planned smelting capacity cuts are driving bullish price trends.

title: Century Aluminum sees higher demand, restarts capacity, and plans 750,000 tonne smelter
summary: Century Aluminum, with total production capacity of 770,000 tonnes per year, produced approximately 638,000 tonnes of primary aluminum in 2025. The company is responding to higher demand by restarting capacity and planning expansion.

title: Global Primary Aluminum Output Hits Historic High!
summary: The International Aluminum Institute reports that primary aluminum production reached a record-breaking high in October 2024, marking a significant milestone for global aluminum output.

title: Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum that could slash industrial costs and accelerate the energy transition
summary: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with innovative properties that could redefine production processes and significantly reduce dependence on critical metals. This breakthrough technology has the potential to substantially lower industrial costs while supporting the energy transition.

title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
summary: Ball Corporation achieved record results in 2025 with significant sustainability improvements in its aluminum operations. The company reported that 74% of aluminum used in its global beverage packaging business came from recycled sources, while 84% met sustainability metrics.

title: Alcoa announces USD 65 million investment to enhance aluminum production
summary: Alcoa Corporation, one of the world's most sustainable aluminum producers, announced a USD 65 million investment to enhance its aluminum production capabilities. This investment reflects the company's commitment to advancing its operations.

title: HRC Steel Falls to 1,129.04 USD/T
summary: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel declined 0.52% to 1,129.04 USD/T on May 12, 2026. Despite the daily decline, HRC Steel has gained 5% over the past 4 weeks and reached its highest level since January 2024 at 1,135.00 USD/T.

title: Steel Prices Rise 0.06% to 3,211 CNY/T
summary: Steel prices in China rose to 3,211 CNY/T with a 0.06% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have climbed 4.19%, and are up 2.36% compared to the same period last year.

title: Steel Market Update: Prices Increase $5-25 per Short Ton
summary: Steel prices increased between $5-25 per short ton from the previous week and are $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago. The market continues to show upward momentum in pricing.

title: United States Steel Corporation Announces Direct Reduction Plant Construction
summary: United States Steel Corporation has announced plans for a new direct reduction plant with an expected construction timeline of approximately 30 months. This represents a significant investment in the company's production capacity.

title: Jindal, thyssenkrupp Halt Sale Talks
summary: thyssenkrupp AG and Jindal International Steel have ended discussions regarding the sale of thyssenkrupp's steel business, citing changed market conditions. Both parties indicated that conditions have improved since initial negotiations began.

title: US Raw Steel Production and Capability Utilization Report
summary: In the week ending May 9, 2026, domestic raw steel production reached 1,880,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 81.4 percent. This represents continued growth in US steel production capacity.

title: US Steel Mill Outages Unlikely to Shift Market
summary: Steel mill outages through the remainder of 2026 are expected to remove nearly 600,000 short tons of flat-rolled production capacity. Industry analysts believe these outages will have limited impact on overall market dynamics.

title: United States Steel Corporation Announces Direct Reduction Plant Construction
summary: United States Steel Corporation has announced a new direct reduction plant with approximately 30 months of construction time expected. This represents a significant investment in advanced steelmaking technology and sustainable production methods.

title: thyssenkrupp and Jindal End Steel Business Sale Talks
summary: thyssenkrupp AG and Jindal International Steel have ended discussions regarding the sale of thyssenkrupp's steel business, citing changed market conditions. The decision reflects evolving dynamics in the global steel industry.

title: The Future of Steel Production: Innovations and Sustainability in the Global Steel Industry
summary: The steel industry is undergoing transformation driven by innovation and sustainability commitments. Key developments include hydrogen-based steelmaking, advanced automation, and green steel technologies that promise a more efficient and environmentally responsible future.

title: Copper Climbs Toward Record High as Global Supply Tightens
summary: Copper prices continue to surge towards record levels as global supply concerns intensify. The commodity has reached fresh all-time highs driven by stronger Chinese demand and growing supply constraints in the market.

title: Copper Futures Reach Fresh All-Time Highs
summary: Copper futures climbed to around $6.6 per pound, reaching fresh all-time highs on the back of stronger Chinese demand and growing supply concerns. The rally reflects both increased global demand and tightening supply conditions.

title: Copper Prices Continue to Surge Towards Record Levels
summary: Copper prices continue their upward trajectory towards record levels as market conditions remain favorable. Multiple sources report sustained strength in copper markets driven by supply and demand dynamics.

title: Copper Climbs Toward Record High as Global Supply Tightens
summary: Copper prices continue to surge towards record levels as global supply constraints tighten the market. The commodity is approaching record highs driven by supply concerns and increased demand.

title: Glencore's African Copper Output Surges 68% Year-on-Year
summary: Glencore's own-sourced copper output from its African assets KCC and Mutanda in the Democratic Republic of Congo increased significantly by 68% year-on-year to 67,900 tonnes. This represents a major boost to the company's copper production capacity.

title: Barrick's Zambia Copper Expansion Highlighted
summary: Barrick Gold's Lumwana copper expansion project in Zambia has been highlighted as a key initiative to increase copper production capacity. The expansion aims to capture more value from rising copper prices.

title: First Quantum Minerals' La Granja Project Reveals 44 Million Tonne Copper Orebody
summary: First Quantum Minerals' mineral resource estimate for its La Granja project in Peru reveals a 44 million tonne contained copper orebody following 370km of drilling. This discovery represents significant copper production potential.

title: Copper prices hit record highs as clean energy and AI fuel global demand
summary: The global copper market is experiencing unprecedented activity with prices surging above $12,000 per ton, the highest level ever recorded. The surge is driven by demand from clean energy projects and artificial intelligence infrastructure development.

title: AI Boom Pushes Copper Toward a Supply Crunch, BNEF Warns
summary: A BloombergNEF report warns that AI data center construction could increase copper demand by 400,000 tonnes annually, with prices potentially reaching $13,500 per tonne by 2028. The report highlights a projected 6 million ton copper deficit by 2028 due to combined demand from data centers, energy transition projects, and traditional manufacturing.

title: Renewables and EVs Are Driving Copper Shortages Faster Than Expected
summary: Recent declines in copper prices may be temporary as soaring demand from renewable energy and electric vehicle markets could quickly convert current surplus into shortage. The accelerating demand from these sectors is outpacing supply growth.

title: Breaking down sudden surge of nickel prices
summary: The London Metal Exchange suspended nickel trading after prices more than doubled to surpass $100,000 per metric ton due to extreme price moves in Asian trading hours. The LME stated that trading would be suspended for at least the remainder of the day.

title: Nickel Price Update: Q1 2026 in Review
summary: Nickel prices spiked at the end of 2025 and faced volatility in Q1 2026, trading within a range of $17,000 to $18,800 per metric ton. The global market is forecast to remain in surplus around 261,000 MT in 2026, with soft demand and elevated supply threatening to send prices lower.

title: Nickel rose to 19,097.75 USD/T
summary: Nickel reached $19,097.75 per metric ton on May 13, 2026, up 0.91% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has risen 4.88% and is up 20.87% year-to-date.

title: Nickel surges after new mine disruptions and copper reaches record highs
summary: Nickel reached a 15-month high above $18,000 per metric ton due to Indonesia's mine production curbs, jumping 6.1% to $18,045 per ton. This represents the highest price since October 7, 2024, as Indonesia implements plans to reduce output with miners able to refer to previously approved quotas for 2026 until March 31.

title: Nickel still capped by surplus
summary: The nickel market is forecast to remain in surplus through 2026 with 261kt surplus expected, driven primarily by continued supply growth from Indonesia. Nickel prices are likely to remain range-bound at an average of $15,250/t in 2026, constrained by elevated inventories and subdued demand, though Indonesian supply disruptions pose upside risks.

title: A 71% production cut at the world's biggest nickel mine - NC1 owns one of the biggest undeveloped nickel projects globally
summary: Indonesia has implemented nickel production quotas for 2026 of 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes previously. NC1 owns one of the top four largest undeveloped nickel projects globally with a JORC resource of approximately 1.68 million tonnes of nickel.

title: Indonesian supply cuts blowing strong wind in nickel's back
summary: Macquarie's commodity desk suspects Indonesia's aim is to cut annual nickel production by 10% through new supply reduction measures. The supply cuts are providing significant support to nickel prices amid market pressures.

title: New EV battery recycling process recovers 99% pure nickel and cobalt
summary: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in the EV battery supply chain by enabling high-purity material recovery from end-of-life batteries.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been instructed to drastically reduce production from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026. The Indonesian government is implementing this cut to boost global nickel prices for its primary export commodity.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali posts lower income for 2023 amid difficult macroeconomic situation
summary: Italian stainless steel long product producer Cogne Acciai Speciali announced its 2023 financial results, registering total income of €822 million, down 15% year-on-year. The company's EBITDA amounted to more than €42 million, reflecting challenging macroeconomic conditions.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires controlling stake in scrap supplier Com.Steel Inox
source: Multiple sources (SteelOrbis, GMK Center, Cogne official)
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali, a subsidiary of Taiwan's Walsin Lihwa, signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, a stainless steel scrap business. The transaction was subject to regulatory approvals and expected to close in the coming months.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali: 94% of energy from renewable sources, 93% permanent employment
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali highlighted its sustainability initiatives, reporting that 94% of energy used comes from renewable sources and 93% of employees have permanent contracts, positioning itself as a conscious industrial player.

title: Tenaris enters into definitive agreement to acquire GLGH Steel, LLC
summary: Tenaris has announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of GLGH Steel, LLC, a U.S.-based company. This acquisition represents a strategic expansion of Tenaris's operations in the steel sector.

title: Tenaris receives ninth consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions recognition
summary: Tenaris has been recognized as a worldsteel Sustainability Champion for the ninth consecutive time. This recognition highlights the company's commitment to sustainable practices in steel production.

title: Tenaris announces $11 million USD digitalization and automation investment
summary: Tenaris has announced a significant investment of $11 million USD in digitalization and automation initiatives. This investment aims to enhance operational efficiency and modernize the company's facilities.

title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel
summary: Italy's Antitrust authority has opened an investigation against major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian, for allegedly participating in anti-competitive agreements involving price fixing and coordinated sales practices since 2005. The investigation involves nine companies and the Italian Association of Electrical Cable and Conductor Industries (AICE). Prysmian's stock price declined 1.26% following the news.

title: Italian Competition Authority: Investigation Launched into Alleged Cartel in the Copper Cable Manufacturing Sector
summary: The Italian Competition Authority formally initiated proceedings against nine companies including Prysmian Cavi e Sistemi Italia and the AICE Association for an alleged agreement restricting competition in the production and sale of low-voltage copper cables. The investigation is in its early stages but could have significant repercussions on the Italian copper cable market.
